content:
1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (one of the descriptors in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List them with `ls .claude/tools/` and run `<path> --help` to learn each tool's purpose and usage. Invoke them via the Bash tool. Project MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning. Useful when you need a second opinion or a paper-style proof sketch the Lean side can then formalize.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (see "Subagents" below). One wrapper handles every subagent.
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`. Each starts with YAML frontmatter (`name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`) followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude session reads.
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents yourself.** The plan and review prompts have an auto-generated **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation that lists every enabled descriptor with its description, write-domain hint, and any `[MANDATORY]` flags. When you decide to invoke a specific subagent, read `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` for its full prompt and directive shape.
31	
32	**Invoke any subagent with the generic wrapper** (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, and writes the subagent's report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. It shells out to `archon subagent <name>`, so executions stream through the Archon JSONL log like a phase agent. The dispatch semaphore bounds total concurrent subagent processes by `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` automatically so deeper-nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Always dispatch synchronously** (not via background `Bash` with `run_in_background: true`). The wrapper returns when the subagent has finished and the report is on disk; only then write your session summary. Background dispatch leaves the parent's session permanently summarized as "running in background", which is wrong as soon as the subagent completes.
45	
46	**Mandatory subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` MUST be dispatched at least once during the plan phase (similarly for `[review]`). The catalog tags them `[MANDATORY]`. A post-phase audit warns when a mandatory dispatch is missing.
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All state files are in `.archon/`:
51	
52	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents | Review Agent | User |
53	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
54	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
55	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
56	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
57	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
58	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
59	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<name>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
60	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
61	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log moved to iter/iter-NNN/review.md) | read |
62	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | do not access | do not access | read (last K iters as context) | read |
63	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (last K iters as context) | do not access | do not access | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write (per-attempt detail) | do not access | do not access | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (write-capable subagents may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | per descriptor: write-capable subagents (e.g. refactor) may edit per their declared write-domain (protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); read-only subagents do not edit | do not edit | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
68	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | write-capable when the descriptor's write_domain covers it | do not edit | read |
69	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | write-capable when the descriptor's write_domain covers it | do not access | read |
70	
71	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent (when a `refactor` descriptor is installed) via the generic `archon-subagent.py --name refactor` wrapper with a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
72	
73	## Protected Declarations
74	
75	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
76	
77	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
78	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
79	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
80	
81	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
82	
83	The blueprint uses two active markers.
84	
85	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
86	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
87	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
88	
89	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
90	
91	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
92	
93	## User Interaction
94	
95	Users provide hints in two places:
96	
97	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
98	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
99	
100	## Agent Roles
101	
102	### Plan Agent
103	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
104	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
105	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
106	- Optionally invoke **subagents** (discoverable via `ls .archon/subagents/`) by running `.claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name <name> ...` via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for the canonical ordering and full invocation pattern.
107	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
108	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
109	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
110	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
111	
112	### Prover Agent
113	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
114	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
115	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
116	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
117	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
118	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
119	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
120	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
121	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
122	
123	### Subagents
124	
125	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime — there is no fixed roster baked into this file. Each lives at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter + prompt body). To see what's available **this iteration**, run `ls .archon/subagents/` and read each descriptor's frontmatter for purpose, write-domain hint, and directive shape.
126	
127	Subagents are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. Phase agents (plan, review) invoke them on demand via the generic wrapper:
128	
129	```
130	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name <name> --slug <slug> --directive-file <path> --write-domain '<glob>'
131	```
132	
133	Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. The dispatching agent reads the report when the wrapper returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings.
134	
135	Some descriptors set `can_spawn: true`, meaning that subagent may itself dispatch children; the per-iter dispatch semaphore caps total concurrent processes by `loop.max_parallel` so deep trees stay bounded.
136	
137	### Review Agent
138	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
139	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
140	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
141	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
142	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
143	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
144	
145	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
146	
147	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
148	
149	- **Iter sidecar init**: at iter start, `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` is created so the plan + review agents have a stable destination for their per-iter narrative (`plan.md`, `review.md`, optional `objectives.md`). Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded across iters because per-iter content lives in the sidecars.
150	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
151	
152	`sync_leanok` writes an inner-git commit (`archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so its output is auditable and revertable.

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You are the prover agent in the proving stage. Your job: fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for context on your assigned file — prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas
9	3. Check your `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments — these are file-specific hints from the user
10	4. Before writing Lean code, you **MUST** consult the relevant blueprint chapter. Blueprints contain mathematical proof sketches; your formal proof must align with them. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs, pushing as far as possible
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you cannot fully prove a sorry, replace it with your best attempt — commented-out proof steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with remaining `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` for the stuck parts), but your work must be visible in the code for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to the original bare `sorry` — that wastes all your work.
13	7. Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md` — what you tried, what worked, what's stuck, next steps
14	
15	**Write permissions**: You may only write to your assigned `.lean` file(s) and your `task_results/<file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	Before editing, read `archon-protected.yaml` at the project root. Declarations listed for your file have **frozen signatures** — you may fill their proof bodies but must not rename them, change their types, reorder their arguments, or weaken their hypotheses. Only the mathematician is allowed to edit protected signatures. 
20	
21	## Avoid Early Termination
22	
23	- Do not abandon a proof prematurely
24	- Do not delegate to the next iteration or another prover if it can be solved with more effort
25	- Many complex problems require thousands of lines of Lean code
26	- Do not stop and leave a sorry simply because the proof is long
27	- Task difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave `sorry` placeholders
28	- Only modify the proof corresponding to the task; leave other proofs/declarations untouched
29	- **Decomposition**: Act like a mathematician — systematically break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure if available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each one individually until the entire goal is closed
30	
31	## Task Completion Criteria
32	
33	Your task is NOT complete until ALL of:
34	1. Every `sorry` has been replaced with a complete proof
35	2. Zero axioms introduced
36	3. The file compiles successfully with no errors
37	
38	If you encounter obstacles:
39	- Break the problem into smaller subgoals
40	- Search for relevant Mathlib lemmas more thoroughly
41	- Prove missing helper lemmas yourself
42	- Try alternative proof strategies
43	- Consult the informal proof / blueprint for guidance
44	- Use Web Search to find paper proofs when Mathlib lacks a theorem
45	
46	### When infrastructure is missing or the current route is too hard
47	
48	Do NOT just report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up on a sorry, you must try to find an alternative yourself:
49	
50	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`) — ask: "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only using tools available in Lean 4 Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
51	2. **Try the alternative** — if the informal agent gives you a route, attempt to formalize it.
52	3. **If you still can't solve it**, save what you learned for the plan agent:
53	   - Write the informal agent's alternative proof sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md`
54	   - In your `task_results/<file>.md`, record: what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found (even if unverified). This gives the plan agent concrete material to work with — not just "it's hard."
55	   - A prover that reports "I couldn't prove X, but here's an alternative approach via Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than one that just says "infrastructure missing."
56	
57	## Proof Style
58	
59	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its proof body
60	- Keep edits minimal: do not delete comments or change labels
61	- Do not add unrelated declarations
62	- **Intermediate helper lemmas you introduced** may be modified if they turn out to be incorrect or need adjustment.
63	- Add concise, informative comments above helper lemmas to make later reuse easy
64	
65	## Interpreting Mathlib Tags in PROGRESS.md
66	
67	The Plan Agent will tag suggested Mathlib lemmas to indicate its confidence. Adjust your effort accordingly:
68	- [verified] — The Plan Agent confirmed this exists. 
69	- [expected] — The Plan Agent is guessing this name based on conventions. Do not assume it exists; do a quick `lean_local_search` and pivot immediately if it doesn't.
70	- [gap] — The Plan Agent verified this does NOT exist in Mathlib. Do not waste time searching for it; formalize a workaround or helper lemma.
71	
72	## LSP MCP Tools: Invocation Rules
73	
74	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls**, not shell commands. Their full names start with `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__` (e.g. `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal`, `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`, `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_local_search`). The lean4 skill reference uses the short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) for readability — those are the **same tools**, never standalone shell binaries.
75	
76	- **Always invoke them through your tool-call interface**, the same way you call `Read` or `Grep`.
77	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` / `lean_diagnostic_messages …` — there is no such shell command and it will fail with `command not found`.
78	- As your **first LSP action**, call `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your assigned file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
79	
80	## Search Protocol
81	
82	Follow the search tool priority and query guidance in the lean4 skill reference (`references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`). Key points:
83	
84	1. `lean_local_search` first
85	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — **describe the mathematical content**, not just the name
86	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only
87	4. Never use local file search (find, grep) to locate Mathlib theorems
88	
89	## Missing Lemmas & Impossibility
90	
91	Follow the lean4 skill reference (`references/sorry-filling.md`) for:
92	- **When Mathlib lacks a theorem**: bypass or implement yourself. Web Search for published papers. Never leave a `sorry` just because Mathlib doesn't have it.
93	- **Distinguish impossibility from difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
94	
95	## Tooling Traps & Verification
96	
97	- Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics: When using `lean_multi_attempt`, do not assume a snippet worked just because `diagnostics: []` is empty. Line-scoping quirks mean errors sometimes attach to the enclosing by block. You MUST check that goals_after is empty (or has advanced) to confirm success.
98	- Beware `lean_run_code` with imports: Standalone `lean_run_code` snippets can sometimes silently swallow elaboration errors if they contain import statements. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
99	
100	## Logging
101	
102	Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Use the file name from your assigned `.lean` file (e.g., if you own `Algebra/WLocal.lean`, write to `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`).
103	
104	**Format:**
105	
106	```markdown
107	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
108	
109	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
110	### Attempt 1
111	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
112	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance not available
113	- **Dead end:** Do not try direct case split without IsLocalRing
114	
115	### Attempt 2
116	- **Approach:** Use Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
117	- **Result:** RESOLVED
118	- **Key insight:** Mathlib's PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective bridges the gap
119	
120	## helper_bijective (line 78)
121	### Attempt 1
122	- **Approach:** Split conjunction into injectivity and surjectivity. Prove injectivity.
123	- **Result:** PARTIAL (Branch Closed) — Injectivity closed. Sorry count unchanged because surjectivity remains.
124	- **Next step:** Try PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective for the right branch.
125	- **Relevant lemmas found:** PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective
126	```
127	
128	**Rules:**
129	1. One section per theorem/lemma in your file
130	2. Each attempt records: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps
131	3. Log negative search results (e.g., "Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib")
132	4. The plan agent collects these files and merges them into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`
133	
134	**Read-only references:** Read `task_pending.md` for prior context on your file. Read `task_done.md` if the current problem resembles a completed one. Do not write to either.
135	
136	## Summary Pipeline
137	
138	1. Read `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md` for context from prior sessions
139	2. Read the informal proof / blueprint to understand the proof strategy and lemma decomposition
140	3. Introduce helper lemmas (matching the blueprint's structure) in the `.lean` file
141	4. Replace `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs, ensuring the file compiles without errors
142	5. Do not modify protected declarations listed in `archon-protected.yaml`.
143	6. Use Mathlib theorems when possible. Use Web Search when Mathlib lacks referenced results
144	7. Rely on Lean LSP for diagnostics; use `lake env lean <file>` sparingly for final checks
145	8. Log all explorations in `task_results/<your_file>.md`
146	
147	## End-of-session handoff
148	
149	Before you stop (or when you are done with this round of work):
150	
151	1. Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` with:
152	   - Current result (IN PROGRESS / FAILED) and what you tried
153	   - Any Mathlib lemmas you discovered that are relevant
154	   - Concrete next step for the next session
155	   - Dead-end warnings for approaches that won't work
156	2. Save all file changes (ensure compilation passes, using scoped `sorry` if needed)
157

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Differentials

8	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.Smooth
9	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Proper

11	
12	/-!
13	# Cotangent space at the identity of a group scheme
14	
15	The cotangent space at the identity of a smooth proper geometrically
16	irreducible group scheme `G` over a base field `k`, defined as a
17	`k`-vector space. Mathematically, this is the pullback of the relative
18	cotangent sheaf `Ω_{G/k}` along the identity section `η_G : 𝟙_ ⟶ G`;
19	the iter-130 Lean realisation (Replacement (B), per
20	`analogies/lieAlgebra-rank-bridge.md`) computes it as the base change to
21	`k` of the algebraic Kähler module `Ω[Γ(G, V) / Γ(Spec k, U)]` on a
22	smooth affine chart `V ⊆ G.left` around the identity-section image.
23	
24	This is piece (i.a) of the shared cotangent-vanishing pile (see
25	`blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` § "Piece (i): sub-lemma
26	decomposition for iter-128+ build" and STRATEGY.md § M2.body-pile).
27	
28	This file contains the definition `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` (line 149
29	below), the structural-shape acceptance lemma
30	`cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars` (line 198 below), and the
31	companion rank lemma `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` (line 244
32	below; rank = relative dimension `n` from
33	`[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]`, closed iter-132).
34	
35	## Status (iter-132 close: rank lemma)
36

95	/-- The **cotangent space at the identity** of a smooth proper geometrically
96	irreducible group scheme `G` over `k`, as a `k`-vector space (returned as
97	`ModuleCat k`).
98	
99	Mathematically: the base change to `k` of the algebraic Kähler module
100	`Ω[Γ(G, V) / Γ(Spec k, U)]` of a smooth affine chart `V ⊆ G.left` around
101	the identity-section image. By the forward Jacobian criterion
102	(`Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega`, consuming
103	`Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential`),
104	this Kähler module is free of rank `n` over `Γ(G, V)`, hence its base
105	change is a finitely-generated free `k`-module of rank `n`; the
106	iter-132 companion rank lemma `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` (at
107	line 244 below) pins the rank to `n` from the
108	`[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]` instance.
109	
110	The bracket / Lie-algebra structure is NOT packaged here — only the
111	underlying `k`-module is needed for the rigidity argument. Downstream
112	consumers that need the dual tangent space `𝔤` may take
113	`Module.Dual k (cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G)`.
114	
115	**Construction (iter-130 prover lane; Replacement (B), affine-chart base
116	change).** The body is built as follows:
117	1. The identity section `η_G : 𝟙_ ⟶ G` of the `GrpObj` structure gives
118	   a scheme morphism `ηleft : Spec k ⟶ G.left` (using the definitional
119	   identification `(𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))).left = Spec (.of k)`).
120	2. Take `x₀ := ηleft default ∈ G.left`, the image of the unique point of
121	   `Spec (.of k)` (which is a singleton because `k` is a field, so
122	   `PrimeSpectrum k` is `Unique`).
123	3. By `Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega` (forward Jacobian criterion,
124	   project lemma in `AlgebraicJacobian.Differentials`), there exists an
125	   affine open `V ⊆ G.left` containing `x₀`, an affine open `U ⊆ Spec k`
126	   with `V ≤ G.hom⁻¹U`, and the `appLE` algebra
127	   `Γ(Spec k, U) → Γ(G, V)` exhibits `Ω[Γ(G, V) / Γ(Spec k, U)]` as a
128	   free `Γ(G, V)`-module of rank `n`.
129	4. Restricting `ηleft` to `V` yields a ring map
130	   `ηleft.appLE V ⊤ _ : Γ(G, V) → Γ(Spec k, ⊤)`; composing with the
131	   canonical iso `Γ(Spec k, ⊤) ≅ k` (`Scheme.ΓSpecIso`) gives
132	   `ψV : Γ(G, V) ⟶ CommRingCat.of k`.
133	5. Extension of scalars from `Γ(G, V)` to `k` along `ψV` of the algebraic
134	   Kähler module gives the cotangent space at the identity as a
135	   `k`-module.
136	
137	**Caveat on canonicity.** The chart `V` used in the body is extracted
138	via `Classical.choose` from `smooth_locally_free_omega`'s existential.
139	The resulting `k`-module is therefore non-canonical *as a value*. What
140	the iter-131 body refactor guarantees is that the body's **structural
141	shape** — a base change to `k` of the chart-level Kähler module — is
142	exposed to downstream consumers: after `unfold cotangentSpaceAtIdentity`
143	and delta-reduction of the `let`-bindings, the outer head symbol is
144	`(ModuleCat.extendScalars _).obj (ModuleCat.of _ Ω[_ ⁄ _])`. This is
145	formalized as `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars` below, which
146	the iter-132 rank lemma `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` (line 244
147	below) rewrites against. For the live consumer
148	(rigidity over `k`, via `rigidity_over_kbar`), the existence of a
149	rank-`n` free `k`-module is the only structural content needed, so
150	chart-canonicity is not load-bearing. A canonical (chart-independent)
151	presentation via the stalk-side cotangent `𝔪 / 𝔪²` is the content of
152	the blueprint's `lem:GrpObj_cotangent_bridge` (deferred to iter-130+).
153	
154	This compiles to a `ModuleCat k` with no `sorry`. The rank (`= n`) is
155	now pinned by `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` (line 244 below;
156	closed iter-132). The free/finite structural properties
157	(`Module.Free k`, `Module.Finite k`) are content for follow-up companion
158	lemmas (not yet in this file); the structural-shape accessibility needed
159	by those lemmas is witnessed here by
160	`cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars` (line 198 below). -/
161	noncomputable def cotangentSpaceAtIdentity (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
162	    [CategoryTheory.GrpObj G] {n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom] [IsProper G.hom]
163	    [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom] :
164	    ModuleCat k :=
165	  -- Identity section `η_G : 𝟙_ ⟶ G`; on schemes this is `Spec k ⟶ G.left`.
166	  let ηleft : Spec (.of k) ⟶ G.left := CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]
167	  -- Image in `G.left` of the unique point of `Spec (.of k)` (`Spec` of a field).
168	  let x₀ : G.left := (ConcreteCategory.hom ηleft.base) default
169	  -- `Classical.choose`-chain through the `Prop`-level existential output of
170	  -- `smooth_locally_free_omega`. Each `let` names one component (chart `U`,
171	  -- chart `V`, the appLE inclusion `e`, and `hxV : x₀ ∈ V`) so the outer
172	  -- expression below stays in a pure-term shape — no `Classical.choice`
173	  -- wrapper at the top level (iter-131 body-shape refactor).
174	  let h := Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega (n := n) G.hom x₀
175	  let U : (Spec (.of k)).Opens := h.choose
176	  let h₁ := h.choose_spec
177	  let V : G.left.Opens := h₁.choose
178	  let h₂ := h₁.choose_spec
179	  let e : V ≤ G.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U := h₂.choose
180	  let hxV : x₀ ∈ V := h₂.choose_spec.1
181	  -- The pre-image of `V` under `ηleft` is all of `Spec (.of k)`, since
182	  -- `Spec` of a field has a unique point and that point lies in `V`.
183	  let htop : (⊤ : (Spec (.of k)).Opens) ≤ ηleft ⁻¹ᵁ V := fun s _ => by
184	    rw [Scheme.Hom.mem_preimage, show s = default from Subsingleton.elim _ _]
185	    exact hxV
186	  -- Ring map `ψV : Γ(G.left, V) ⟶ k` from the identity section restricted to
187	  -- `V`, composed with the canonical iso `Γ(Spec k, ⊤) ≅ k`.
188	  let ψV : Γ(G.left, V) ⟶ CommRingCat.of k :=
189	    ηleft.appLE V ⊤ htop ≫ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of k)).hom
190	  -- Equip `Γ(G.left, V)` with the `Γ(Spec k, U)`-algebra structure from
191	  -- `appLE` on `G.hom`.
192	  letI : Algebra ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U) ↥Γ(G.left, V) :=
193	    (Scheme.Hom.appLE G.hom U V e).hom.toAlgebra
194	  -- Body: base-change the algebraic Kähler module `Ω[Γ(G.left, V) / Γ(Spec k, U)]`
195	  -- from `Γ(G.left, V)` to `k` along `ψV`. By `smooth_locally_free_omega`
196	  -- (consuming `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential`)
197	  -- this Kähler module is free of rank `n` over `Γ(G.left, V)`, hence its
198	  -- base change is free of rank `n` over `k`.
199	  (ModuleCat.extendScalars ψV.hom).obj
200	    (ModuleCat.of Γ(G.left, V) Ω[Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ Γ(Spec (.of k), U)])
201	
202	/-- Defensive lemma: `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G` is definitionally equal to its
203	explicit chart-base-changed Kähler module form. The chart `V`, the algebra
204	structure, and the Kähler module are opaque (`Classical.choose`-extracted) but
205	the outer `extendScalars`/`ModuleCat.of`-of-`Ω` form is exposed. This proves
206	that the iter-131 body refactor closed the iter-130 opacity defect: a
207	downstream rank lemma can `simp only [cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars]`
208	(combined with a `Classical.choose`-chain `obtain` on the same existential)
209	to rewrite the body to the explicit form. -/
210	theorem cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
211	    [CategoryTheory.GrpObj G] {n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]
212	    [IsProper G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom] :
213	    ∃ (U : (Spec (.of k)).Opens) (V : G.left.Opens) (e : V ≤ G.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U)
214	        (htop : (⊤ : (Spec (.of k)).Opens) ≤
215	          (CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]) ⁻¹ᵁ V),
216	      letI : Algebra ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U) ↥Γ(G.left, V) :=
217	        (Scheme.Hom.appLE G.hom U V e).hom.toAlgebra
218	      (cotangentSpaceAtIdentity (n := n) G : ModuleCat k) =
219	        (ModuleCat.extendScalars
220	          ((CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]).appLE V ⊤ htop ≫
221	            (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of k)).hom).hom).obj
222	          (ModuleCat.of Γ(G.left, V) Ω[Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ Γ(Spec (.of k), U)]) := by
223	  let ηleft : Spec (.of k) ⟶ G.left := CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]
224	  let x₀ : G.left := (ConcreteCategory.hom ηleft.base) default
225	  let h := Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega (n := n) G.hom x₀
226	  let hxV : x₀ ∈ h.choose_spec.choose := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.1
227	  refine ⟨h.choose, h.choose_spec.choose, h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose,
228	    fun s _ => ?_, rfl⟩
229	  change (ConcreteCategory.hom ηleft.base) s ∈ h.choose_spec.choose
230	  rw [show s = default from Subsingleton.elim _ _]
231	  exact hxV
232	
233	/-- **Rank lemma for the cotangent space at the identity.**
234	
235	For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme `G` over `k` of
236	relative dimension `n`, the `k`-vector space `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G` has
237	`finrank` equal to `n`.
238	
239	**Proof strategy (per `blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` §
240	`lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra_finrank`, Steps 1+2 live closure path).**
241	
242	* **Step 1 (chart-side Kähler rank).** Reproduce the iter-131 body's
243	  `Classical.choose`-chain on `Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega` to extract
244	  the same chart witnesses `U`, `V`, `e` plus `hfree : Module.Free Γ(G, V)
245	  Ω[Γ(G, V) ⁄ Γ(Spec k, U)]` and `hrank : Module.rank ... = n`. Apply
246	  `Module.finrank_eq_of_rank_eq hrank` to get
247	  `Module.finrank Γ(G, V) Ω[…] = n`.
248	
249	* **Step 2 (base-change preserves finrank).** The body of
250	  `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` is by construction the `extendScalars ψ_V`
251	  base change of `Ω[Γ(G, V) ⁄ Γ(Spec k, U)]` from `Γ(G, V)` to `k`. Apply
252	  `Module.finrank_baseChange` to push `Module.finrank Γ(G, V) Ω[…]` to
253	  `Module.finrank k (k ⊗_{Γ(G,V)} Ω[…])`, discharging the `Module.Free`
254	  hypothesis via `hfree` and the `Nontrivial Γ(G, V)` hypothesis from the
255	  existence of the ring map `ψ_V : Γ(G, V) → k` into the field `k`. -/
256	theorem cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
257	    [CategoryTheory.GrpObj G] {n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]
258	    [IsProper G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom] :
259	    Module.finrank k (cotangentSpaceAtIdentity (n := n) G) = n := by
260	  -- Reproduce the same `Classical.choose`-chain as the body of `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity`.
261	  let ηleft : Spec (.of k) ⟶ G.left := CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]
262	  let x₀ : G.left := (ConcreteCategory.hom ηleft.base) default
263	  let h := Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega (n := n) G.hom x₀
264	  let U : (Spec (.of k)).Opens := h.choose
265	  let V : G.left.Opens := h.choose_spec.choose
266	  let e : V ≤ G.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose
267	  have hxV : x₀ ∈ V := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.1
268	  -- Algebra structure on `Γ(G.left, V)` over `Γ(Spec k, U)` (matches the body's `letI`).
269	  letI algGV : Algebra ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U) ↥Γ(G.left, V) :=
270	    (Scheme.Hom.appLE G.hom U V e).hom.toAlgebra
271	  -- Freeness + rank witnesses from the existential.
272	  have hfree : Module.Free ↥Γ(G.left, V) Ω[↥Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U)] :=
273	    h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.2.2.2.1
274	  have hrank : Module.rank ↥Γ(G.left, V) Ω[↥Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U)] = (n : Cardinal) :=
275	    h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.2.2.2.2
276	  -- ψV ring map (matches the body's `ψV`).
277	  have htop : (⊤ : (Spec (.of k)).Opens) ≤ ηleft ⁻¹ᵁ V := fun s _ => by
278	    rw [Scheme.Hom.mem_preimage, show s = default from Subsingleton.elim _ _]
279	    exact hxV
280	  let ψV : Γ(G.left, V) ⟶ CommRingCat.of k :=
281	    ηleft.appLE V ⊤ htop ≫ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of k)).hom
282	  letI algGVk : Algebra ↥Γ(G.left, V) k := ψV.hom.toAlgebra
283	  -- The Γ(G, V) algebra is Nontrivial because it maps to the field `k`.
284	  haveI : Nontrivial ↥Γ(G.left, V) := ψV.hom.domain_nontrivial
285	  -- The body of `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` is `(extendScalars ψV.hom).obj (ModuleCat.of … Ω[…])`,
286	  -- whose carrier is `TensorProduct Γ(G, V) k Ω[…]` (under `algGVk`). Definitional equality
287	  -- of the carriers reduces the goal to `Module.finrank k (TensorProduct ...) = n`.
288	  change Module.finrank k (TensorProduct ↥Γ(G.left, V) k
289	      (Ω[↥Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U)])) = n
290	  -- Step 2: Base-change preserves finrank.
291	  rw [Module.finrank_baseChange (R := k) (S := ↥Γ(G.left, V))
292	        (M' := Ω[↥Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U)])]
293	  -- Step 1: Apply rank=n ⇒ finrank=n.
294	  exact Module.finrank_eq_of_rank_eq hrank
295

13	by translation? Two sub-questions matter:

43	### Decision 1: Shear iso `σ = ⟨pr₁, μ⟩ : G ⊗ G ≅ G ⊗ G` — Mathlib precedent
44	
45	- **Mathlib idiom**: NO packaged shear-iso for the binary product. The
46	  closest precedents are:
47	  - `CategoryTheory.GrpObj.mulRight`
48	    (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Grp_.lean:277`): an `A ≅ A`
49	    automorphism `lift (𝟙 _) (toUnit _ ≫ f) ≫ μ` where `f : 𝟙_ ⟶ A`
50	    is a global element. Defined for any `GrpObj A` in any
51	    `CartesianMonoidalCategory C`. **This is *not* the binary-product
52	    shear** — it has only one free input, not two — but it uses the
53	    structurally identical idiom `lift _ _ ≫ μ`.
54	  - `CategoryTheory.GrpObj.isPullback`
55	    (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Grp_.lean:293`): the associativity
56	    diagram `μ ▷ A ⋯ μ` is Cartesian. The pullback-cone construction
57	    at lines 296-323 internally uses exactly the σ/τ shear-style
58	    `lift … (lift … ι …) ≫ μ` construction, so all categorical
59	    machinery (`lift_lift_assoc`, `lift_comp_inv_left`,
60	    `eq_lift_inv_right`, …) lives in this file already.
61	  - `Homeomorph.shearMulRight`
62	    (`Mathlib.Topology.Algebra.Group.Basic.lean:632`): the type-level
63	    shear for *topological groups* (`(x,y) ↦ (x, x*y)`, `G × G ≃ₜ G × G`).
64	    Requires actual `[Group G]` typeclass on the carrier, not `GrpObj`
65	    in a category. Used only in the alg-closed-base branch of
66	    `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.Smooth.lean:50-51` via
67	    `pointEquivClosedPoint` — exactly the construction the iter-127
68	    over-k risk register flags as forbidden.
69	
70	- **Project's proposed path** (per blueprint `RigidityKbar.tex:243-268`):
71	  build `σ_hom := lift (fst G G) μ : G ⊗ G ⟶ G ⊗ G` and
72	  `σ_inv := lift (fst G G) (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) (snd G G) ≫ μ)` (= the
73	  `τ = ⟨pr₁, μ ∘ (ι × id)⟩` of the sketch), in `Over (Spec (.of k))`,
74	  from `GrpObj` data only. Verify `σ_hom ≫ σ_inv = 𝟙` and
75	  `σ_inv ≫ σ_hom = 𝟙` via the `lift_*` calculus.
76	
77	- **Gap**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL. Mathlib has all building blocks
78	  (`lift`, `μ`, `ι`, `lift_lift_assoc`, `lift_comp_inv_left`,
79	  `lift_comp_inv_right`) and the precedent `isPullback` shows the exact
80	  proof shape, but the *packaged* `A ⊗ A ≅ A ⊗ A` shear iso is not
81	  shipped. The project must build it.

255	  \emph{Step 2: base-change to $k$ preserves $\finrank$.} By construction, the body of \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace} is the base change $k \otimes_{\Gamma(G, V)} \Omega_{\Gamma(G, V)/\Gamma(\Spec k, U)}$ along the ring map $\psi_V \colon \Gamma(G, V) \to k$ obtained from the identity section restricted to $V$ (composed with $\Gamma(\Spec k, \cdot) \cong k$). The Mathlib base-change lemma
256	  \[
257	    \texttt{Module.finrank\_baseChange} \colon\;\;
258	    \texttt{Module.finrank}\;R\;(\texttt{TensorProduct}\;S\;R\;M')
259	      \;=\; \texttt{Module.finrank}\;S\;M'
260	  \]
261	  (\texttt{Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Dimension.Constructions}; requires $S$ a commutative semiring with \texttt{StrongRankCondition}, $M'$ a \texttt{Module.Free}\;$S$-module, and $R$ an $S$-algebra with \texttt{StrongRankCondition}), applied with $S := \Gamma(G, V)$, $R := k$, and $M' := \Omega_{\Gamma(G, V)/\Gamma(\Spec k, U)}$ — using \texttt{hfree} from Step 1 to discharge the \texttt{Module.Free} hypothesis — yields
262	  \[
263	    \finrank_{k}\,\mathfrak g^{\vee} \;=\; \finrank_{k}\bigl(k \otimes_{\Gamma(G, V)} \Omega_{\Gamma(G, V)/\Gamma(\Spec k, U)}\bigr) \;=\; \finrank_{\Gamma(G, V)}\,\Omega_{\Gamma(G, V)/\Gamma(\Spec k, U)} \;=\; n,
264	  \]
265	  which closes the rank lemma on the live (B) path.

267	  \emph{Companion freeness fact (downstream consumer; not on the rank-lemma critical path).} For downstream consumers that separately need $k$-freeness of $\mathfrak g^{\vee}$ — notably \cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_free} of piece (i.c) — the typeclass \texttt{Algebra.TensorProduct.instFree} (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.TensorProduct.Free}) supplies $\texttt{Module.Free}\;k\;(k \otimes_{\Gamma(G, V)} \Omega_{\Gamma(G, V)/\Gamma(\Spec k, U)})$ directly from \texttt{hfree}. This is informational here; the rank lemma above closes without invoking it.

The route is: (i) bridge from the chart-base-changed body to the local-stalk Kähler module via \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangent_bridge} (currently \texttt{\textbackslash notready}; identifies $k \otimes_{\Gamma(G, V)} \Omega_{\Gamma(G, V)/\Gamma(\Spec k, U)} \cong \mathfrak m_{\eta_G}/\mathfrak m_{\eta_G}^{2}$, via a localisation-at-the-identity-stalk step composed with the standard $k \otimes_{R} \Omega_{R/k} \cong \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^{2}$ identification — Stacks Tag~02G1 / Hartshorne~II.8.7); (ii) invoke regularity of $\mathcal O_{G, \eta_G}$ (\texttt{IsRegularLocalRing}; from smoothness of $G \to \Spec k$ at the closed point $\eta_G$, Stacks Tag~00TR / Hartshorne~II.10.3.B), with Krull dimension of $\mathcal O_{G, \eta_G}$ equal to the relative dimension $n$ (Stacks Tag~02G1); (iii) read off the rank from the defining property of regular local rings, $\finrank_{k}\,\mathfrak m_{\eta_G}/\mathfrak m_{\eta_G}^{2} = \dim \mathcal O_{G, \eta_G} = n$, packaged into \texttt{Ideal.IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace} (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.Cotangent}) and \texttt{IsRegularLocalRing} (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.RegularLocalRing.Defs}). This route is preferred only when a downstream consumer requires the canonical (chart-independent) stalk-side presentation; it is \emph{not} on the live closure path under Replacement (B) because the bridge lemma \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangent_bridge} is \texttt{\textbackslash notready}. The footer paragraph below records the iter-131 strategy-critic verdict on this deferral.

272	  \[
273	    \finrank_{k}\,\mathfrak g \;=\; \finrank_{k}\,\mathrm{Hom}_{k}(\mathfrak g^{\vee}, k) \;=\; \finrank_{k}\,\mathfrak g^{\vee} \;=\; n,
274	  \]
275	  delivering the consequent $\finrank_{k}\,\mathfrak g = n$ on the tangent side.

324	  Let $\mu \colon G \times_{k} G \to G$ denote the multiplication of $G$ and let $\sigma \colon G \times_{k} G \to G \times_{k} G$ be the \emph{shear isomorphism}
325	  \[
326	    \sigma \;=\; \langle \pr_{1},\, \mu \rangle, \qquad (a, b) \;\mapsto\; (a,\, a \cdot b),
327	  \]
328	  built categorically from $\mu$ and the binary-product projections in $\Over\,(\Spec k)$. Then $\sigma$ is an isomorphism over the first projection, and pullback along $\sigma$ together with the canonical first-projection-cotangent identification $\Omega_{(G \times_{k} G)/G} \cong \pr_{2}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k}$ (\cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_basechange_proj}) yields a natural isomorphism of presheaves of modules on $G$
329	  \[

331	  \]
332	  where $\pi_{G} \colon G \to \Spec k$ is the structure map (in the standard category-theory notation for the binary product in $\Over\,(\Spec k)$, this is the "first projection" of the singleton-product-with-base, sometimes written $\pr_{1}$; we use $\pi_{G}$ to avoid confusion with the binary-product first projection $\pr_{1} \colon G \times_{k} G \to G$ appearing above). Informally: the relative cotangent of $G$ is canonically isomorphic to the constant $\mathcal O_{G}$-module with fibre $\eta_{G}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k}$, trivialised along the structure map.

334	  \paragraph{Iter-127 over-k risk register.} The globalisation \textbf{must} be formulated functorially via the shear scheme map $\sigma$ — which is a morphism in $\Over\,(\Spec k)$, valid for any base field — and \textbf{not} as a pointwise translation by closed (or $\bar k$-rational) points of $G$. The pointwise-translation argument requires either alg-closure of $k$ or a Nullstellensatz-style $\bar k$-point lifting (as in Mathlib's \texttt{pointEquivClosedPoint}, used in the alg-closed-base branch of \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.Smooth.lean:50--51}); the functorial shear formulation sidesteps that obstacle entirely. The iter-133 mathlib-analogist verdict (\texttt{analogies/mulright-globalises-cotangent.md} Decision 4) explicitly rules out the pointwise idioms of \texttt{Mathlib.Geometry.Manifold.GroupLieAlgebra.mulInvariantVectorField} (requires \texttt{[Group G]}) and \texttt{Mathlib.Topology.Algebra.Group.Basic.Homeomorph.shearMulRight} (requires \texttt{[Group G]} on the carrier) as the over-k-forbidden alternatives.
335	
336	  \paragraph{Lean target name vs.\ construction (option (i), iter-133).} The named target \texttt{mulRight\_globalises\_cotangent} retains the \texttt{mulRight} naming convention because the shear iso $\sigma = \langle \pr_{1}, \mu \rangle$ on $G \times_{k} G$ is the binary-product upgrade of \texttt{CategoryTheory.GrpObj.mulRight} (\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Grp\_.lean:277--281}, which packages a one-input automorphism $a \mapsto a \cdot f$ for a fixed global element $f \colon \mathbf{1} \to G$): the map $\sigma$ parametrises the family of right-translations $\{\mathrm{mulRight}\,a\}_{a \in G}$ globally across $G$ by taking the first coordinate as the parameter, and the second projection $\pr_{2} \circ \sigma = \mu$ realises the right-translation action on the second coordinate. The "globalises the cotangent" phrasing in the name records that this family-of-translations is what triviallises $\Omega_{G/k}$ via pullback along $\sigma$ (cf.\ the inverse-pair proof shape of \texttt{CategoryTheory.GrpObj.isPullback}, \texttt{Grp\_.lean:293--323}, which uses the structurally identical \texttt{lift \dots (lift \dots \,\iota\, \dots) ≫ μ} construction). No standalone \texttt{mulRight}-name function appears in the Lean signature itself; the connection is purely nomenclatural, and the underlying Lean construction is the binary-product shear iso, not a one-input \texttt{mulRight} automorphism.
337	\end{lemma}
338	
339	\begin{proof}
340	  \uses{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace, lem:GrpObj_omega_basechange_proj, lem:GrpObj_omega_restrict_to_identity_section}
341	
342	  \emph{Step 1: Build the shear iso $\sigma$.} Define
343	  \[
344	    \sigma_{\mathrm{hom}} \;:=\; \langle \pr_{1},\, \mu \rangle \;=\; \texttt{lift\,(fst\,G\,G)\,μ} \colon G \times_{k} G \to G \times_{k} G
345	  \]
346	  in $\Over\,(\Spec k)$ from the $\GrpObj$ data only. The candidate inverse
347	  \[
348	    \sigma_{\mathrm{inv}} \;:=\; \langle \pr_{1},\, \mu \circ (\iota \circ \pr_{1},\, \pr_{2}) \rangle \;=\; \texttt{lift\,(fst\,G\,G)\,(lift\,(fst\,G\,G\,≫\,$\iota$)\,(snd\,G\,G)\,≫\,μ)},
349	  \]
350	  where $\iota \colon G \to G$ is the inverse on $G$, is again a scheme map in $\Over\,(\Spec k)$ — informally, $(a, b) \mapsto (a, a^{-1} \cdot b)$. One verifies $\sigma_{\mathrm{hom}} \circ \sigma_{\mathrm{inv}} = \mathrm{id}_{G \times_{k} G} = \sigma_{\mathrm{inv}} \circ \sigma_{\mathrm{hom}}$ using the $\GrpObj$ axioms (associativity and left/right inverse) via the Mathlib calculus \texttt{lift\_lift\_assoc}, \texttt{lift\_comp\_inv\_left}, \texttt{lift\_comp\_inv\_right}, and \texttt{comp\_lift\_assoc} — the same idioms used by \texttt{CategoryTheory.GrpObj.mulRight} and \texttt{CategoryTheory.GrpObj.isPullback} (\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Grp\_.lean:277--281,\,293--323}). Estimated build: $\sim 30$--$60$ LOC. This is a NEEDS\_MATHLIB\_GAP\_FILL (no packaged $A \otimes A \cong A \otimes A$ binary-product shear iso exists in Mathlib, although the building blocks all do; per the iter-133 mathlib-analogist verdict Decision 1).
351	
352	  \emph{Step 2: Base-change of differentials.} View $G \times_{k} G$ as a $G$-scheme via the first projection $\pr_{1}$. The relative cotangent of this $G$-scheme, $\Omega_{(G \times_{k} G)/G}$, is canonically isomorphic to the pullback $\pr_{2}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k}$ via \cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_basechange_proj} (the load-bearing helper sub-lemma, NEEDS\_MATHLIB\_GAP\_FILL, $\sim 150$--$300$ LOC):
353	  \[
354	    \Omega_{(G \times_{k} G)/G} \;\cong\; \pr_{2}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k} \qquad \text{on } G \times_{k} G.
355	  \]
356	  Since $\sigma_{\mathrm{hom}}$ is an iso over $\pr_{1}$ (i.e.\ $\pr_{1} \circ \sigma_{\mathrm{hom}} = \pr_{1}$, by construction of $\sigma_{\mathrm{hom}} = \langle \pr_{1}, \mu \rangle$ via the binary-product universal property), $\sigma$ acts as a self-iso of $G \times_{k} G$ as a $G$-scheme via $\pr_{1}$; functoriality of $\Omega_{(\cdot)/G}$ under such self-isos then re-routes the Step 2 iso through $\sigma$ to relate the differentials at the two projections, with $\pr_{2} \circ \sigma_{\mathrm{hom}} = \mu$.
357	
358	  \emph{Step 3: Restrict along the section $s := \langle \mathrm{id}_{G},\, \eta_{G} \rangle \colon G \to G \times_{k} G$.} The section $s$ satisfies $\pr_{1} \circ s = \mathrm{id}_{G}$ and $\pr_{2} \circ s = \eta_{G} \circ \pi_{G}$ in $\Over\,(\Spec k)$, where $\pi_{G} \colon G \to \Spec k$ is the structure map. Applying $s^{*}$ to both sides of the Step 2 iso $\Omega_{(G \times_{k} G)/G} \cong \pr_{2}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k}$:
359	  \begin{itemize}
360	    \item The LHS $s^{*}(\Omega_{(G \times_{k} G)/G})$ is, by the $G$-scheme structure given by $\pr_{1}$ and the section identity $\pr_{1} \circ s = \mathrm{id}_{G}$, canonically isomorphic to $\Omega_{G/k}$ (restricting the relative cotangent of $G \times_{k} G$ over $G$ to a section of the $G$-projection recovers $\Omega_{G/k}$ — a standard relative-cotangent identification for a section of a smooth morphism). Mathlib-wise this combines \texttt{PresheafOfModules.pullbackComp} with \texttt{PresheafOfModules.pullbackId} on the composition $\pr_{1} \circ s = \mathrm{id}_{G}$.
361	    \item The RHS $s^{*}(\pr_{2}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k})$ is, by \cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_restrict_to_identity_section} applied to the section identity $\pr_{2} \circ s = \eta_{G} \circ \pi_{G}$, canonically isomorphic to $\pi_{G}^{*}(\eta_{G}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k})$.
362	  \end{itemize}
363	  Composing the LHS and RHS identifications with the pulled-back Step 2 iso yields the displayed iso $\Omega_{G/k} \cong \pi_{G}^{*}(\eta_{G}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k})$. Estimated build: $\sim 30$--$80$ LOC (per the iter-133 mathlib-analogist verdict Decision 4, step 3 of the LOC envelope). The role of $\sigma$ in this proof is the Step 2 functorial book-keeping — it pins the "right-translation" interpretation of the trivialisation (the second-coordinate cotangent $\pr_{2}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k}$ is identified with the first-projection cotangent $\Omega_{(G \times_{k} G)/G}$ via Step 2's helper, and $\sigma$ realises the consistency of these two views as a single $G$-scheme structure on $G \times_{k} G$).
364	
365	  \emph{Mathlib name summary.} Step 1 (shear iso): \texttt{CategoryTheory.GrpObj.mulRight} (template; \texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Grp\_.lean:277--281}) + \texttt{CategoryTheory.GrpObj.isPullback} (inverse-pair proof-shape template; \texttt{Grp\_.lean:293--323}), with the \texttt{lift\_*} calculus (\texttt{lift\_lift\_assoc}, \texttt{lift\_comp\_inv\_left}, \texttt{lift\_comp\_inv\_right}, \texttt{comp\_lift\_assoc}). Step 2 (base-change of differentials): \cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_basechange_proj} (chains \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.tensorKaehlerEquiv} from \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.TensorProduct} with the presheaf-side \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.pullback} from \texttt{Mathlib.Topology.Sheaves.Pullback}, plus the project's \texttt{relativeDifferentialsPresheaf\_obj\_kaehler}; NEEDS\_MATHLIB\_GAP\_FILL, $\sim 150$--$300$ LOC). Step 3 (section restriction): \cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_restrict_to_identity_section} (applies \texttt{PresheafOfModules.pullbackComp} and \texttt{PresheafOfModules.pullbackId} from \texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Pullback}; $\sim 30$--$80$ LOC).
366	
367	  \emph{Total LOC envelope for piece (i.b) under the recommended sheaf-level RHS}: $\sim 210$--$440$ LOC (per the iter-133 mathlib-analogist verdict). The bridge to the value-level $\texttt{cotangentSpaceAtIdentity}\,G$ — a chart-localisation identification of $\sim 100$--$200$ LOC — is a structurally distinct artefact consumed downstream in piece (i.c) (\cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_free}), \textbf{not} inside this lemma's body.
368	\end{proof}

386	  %           (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom)
387	  % where `(G ⊗ G).left` is the underlying scheme of the categorical product
388	  % `G ⊗ G` in `Over (Spec (.of k))` (equivalently, the fibre product
389	  % `G.left ×_{Spec k} G.left`), the LHS uses the first projection
390	  % `pr_1 : G ⊗ G ⟶ G` as the structure morphism for the relative cotangent
391	  % of `G ⊗ G` viewed as a `G`-scheme, and `φ_pr_two` is the compatibility

398	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf, lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}
399	  \notready
400	  Let $G : \Over\,(\Spec k)$ be as above, and consider the binary product $G \times_{k} G$ in $\Over\,(\Spec k)$, with first and second projections $\pr_{1}, \pr_{2} \colon G \times_{k} G \to G$. View $G \times_{k} G$ as a $G$-scheme via $\pr_{1}$. Then there is a canonical natural isomorphism of presheaves of modules on $G \times_{k} G$
401	  \[
402	    \Omega_{(G \times_{k} G)/G} \;\cong\; \pr_{2}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k},
403	  \]
404	  where the left-hand side is the relative cotangent of $G \times_{k} G$ viewed as a $G$-scheme via $\pr_{1}$, and the right-hand side is the pullback along $\pr_{2}$ of $\Omega_{G/k} = \texttt{relativeDifferentialsPresheaf}\,G.\mathrm{hom}$.

407	\begin{proof}
408	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf, lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}
409	  The construction chains two Mathlib idioms. On each affine chart $W = \Spec B$ of the source $G \times_{k} G$, the sections of both sides are Kähler-differential modules with appropriate base rings. The left-hand side $\Omega_{(G \times_{k} G)/G}$ over $W$ gives $\Omega_{B/B_{1}}$, where $B_{1}$ corresponds to the chart of $G$ obtained by restricting $\pr_{1}$ to $W$. The right-hand side $\pr_{2}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k}$ over $W$ gives $B \otimes_{B_{2}} \Omega_{B_{2}/k}$, where $B_{2}$ corresponds to the chart of $G$ obtained by restricting $\pr_{2}$. The binary-product universal property in $\Over\,(\Spec k)$ supplies an \texttt{Algebra.IsPushout}\,$k$\,$B_{1}$\,$B_{2}$\,$B$ square (the two projections $B_{1}, B_{2} \to B$ exhibit $B$ as the tensor product $B_{1} \otimes_{k} B_{2}$ at chart level). The algebra-side base-change identity
410	  \[
411	    \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.tensorKaehlerEquiv} \colon \;\; B \,\otimes_{B_{2}}\, \Omega_{B_{2}/k} \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\; \Omega_{B/B_{1}}
412	  \]
413	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.TensorProduct}; for an \texttt{Algebra.IsPushout R S A B} square, yields $B \otimes_{A} \Omega[A/R] \simeq_{B} \Omega[B/S]$) gives the chart-level value identity. Promoting this chart-by-chart equivalence to a natural iso of presheaves of modules uses the presheaf-side functor \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.pullback} (\texttt{Mathlib.Topology.Sheaves.Pullback}) composed with the project-side chart-level value identity \texttt{relativeDifferentialsPresheaf\_obj\_kaehler} (\cref{lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}, \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean:60}); naturality follows from naturality of \texttt{tensorKaehlerEquiv} and of \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.pullback}.
414	
415	  \emph{Mathlib name summary (NEEDS\_MATHLIB\_GAP\_FILL, $\sim 150$--$300$ LOC).} Algebra-side: \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.tensorKaehlerEquiv} (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.TensorProduct}). Presheaf-side: \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.pullback} (\texttt{Mathlib.Topology.Sheaves.Pullback}) and \texttt{PresheafOfModules.pullback} (\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Pullback}). Project-side: \texttt{relativeDifferentialsPresheaf\_obj\_kaehler} (\cref{lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}, \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean:60--66}). This is the load-bearing piece of (i.b); without it, the shear iso of Step 1 of \cref{lem:GrpObj_mulRight_globalises}'s proof cannot be turned into a cotangent-level iso. Mathlib has no scheme-level relative cotangent sheaf at all (per \texttt{analogies/mulright-globalises-cotangent.md} Decision 2), so this lemma is a genuine NEEDS\_MATHLIB\_GAP\_FILL contribution candidate. Compatible upstream Mathlib PR-shape: a sheaf-of-modules version of \texttt{tensorKaehlerEquiv} promoting \texttt{Algebra.IsPushout}-of-charts to a presheaf-level natural iso.
416	\end{proof}

453	  \label{lem:GrpObj_omega_free}

461	  \uses{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace, lem:GrpObj_mulRight_globalises}

The chart witnesses $U, V, e, h_{xV}, \texttt{hfree}, \texttt{hrank}$ are extracted directly by re-running the same \texttt{Classical.choose}-chain on \cref{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega} inside the consumer's proof body (and $\psi_V$ reconstructed via \texttt{appLE} composition with \texttt{Scheme.$\Gamma$SpecIso}). The closure then reads \texttt{rw [Module.finrank\_baseChange]; exact Module.finrank\_eq\_of\_rank\_eq hrank}. \textbf{This is the shorter route, and what the rank lemma's iter-132 body actually does.} It is the recommended pattern for downstream consumers whose goal can be massaged via \texttt{change} into the underlying \texttt{TensorProduct} carrier.
494	  \item \textbf{\texttt{obtain}+\texttt{rw [heq]} alternative (preserved for consumers needing an explicit rewrite handle).} The companion lemma \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace_extendScalars_witness} supports a fielded destructure \texttt{obtain $\langle$U, V, e, $h_\mathrm{top}$, heq$\rangle$ := cotangentSpaceAtIdentity\_eq\_extendScalars G}, after which \texttt{rw [heq]} exposes the explicit \texttt{extendScalars} form to the goal; the chart-side \texttt{hfree}/\texttt{hrank} are re-extracted on the same \texttt{Classical.choose}-chain from \cref{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega} and fed into \texttt{Module.finrank\_baseChange}. This route makes the rewrite step explicit and is preferred when the consumer's goal does not unify with the body's underlying \texttt{TensorProduct} carrier via \texttt{change} alone (e.g., when the goal is phrased at the \texttt{ModuleCat k}-object level rather than at the underlying-type level).
495	\end{itemize}
496	
497	Note: an alternative interface that would let consumers destructure with a single \texttt{let} (a \texttt{$\Sigma'$}-bundled \texttt{smooth\_locally\_free\_omega'} returning the chart data as a sigma type rather than an existential) is a deferred cleanup opportunity flagged in \texttt{analogies/cotangent-body-shape.md} \S "Future cleanup"; it is out of scope until \texttt{Differentials.lean} comes off the do-not-touch list.
498	
499	\textbf{Honest pile cost (pieces (i)+(ii)+(iii), piece (iv) deferred): 1350--2600 LOC over 7--14 iters.} This is the multi-iter shared build whose iter-range is the dominant cost driver of M2 closure per STRATEGY.md \S Sequencing. The iter-126 analogist's honest decomposition supersedes an earlier "800--1500 LOC / 8--12 iter" framing that conflated piece costs and undercounted piece (i).
500	
501	Per-piece build order: \textbf{piece (i) $\to$ piece (ii) $\to$ piece (iii)}. Piece (i) is the dominant cost and the source of the cotangent-trivial input; pieces (ii) and (iii) consume it. Piece (iv) is parked, not built.
502	
503	\section{Use in the project}
504	
505	The named declaration $\thm{thm:rigidity_over_kbar}$ is consumed by:
506	
507	\begin{itemize}
508	  \item \textbf{M2.b genus-0 witness} (iter-127 scaffold $\thm{def:genusZeroWitness}$, then later body iter-145+). The \texttt{isAlbaneseFor} field of \texttt{JacobianWitness C} for a genus-$0$ curve $C$ over $k$ branches on whether $C(k) \neq \emptyset$. When $C(k) \neq \emptyset$, \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} is invoked \textbf{directly over $k$} (post-iter-127 over-k commitment; no base-change-and-descent step); the marked point $P \in C(k)$ supplied by the universal quantifier feeds into the rigidity declaration's pointing hypothesis. When $C(k) = \emptyset$ (Brauer--Severi conics over $\mathbb Q$ are the standard counterexample), the field is \textbf{vacuously true} at the Lean type level: Lean's $\forall (P : \mathbf{1} \to C), \mathtt{IsAlbanese}\ldots$ is trivially satisfied when the type $\mathbf{1} \to C$ is empty.
509	  \item \textbf{No other in-tree consumer.} The declaration is project-internal; it is not consumed by any \texttt{Cohomology/*.lean}, \texttt{Genus.lean}, \texttt{AbelJacobi.lean}, or \texttt{Differentials.lean} declaration.
510	\end{itemize}

6	import Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.Basic
7	import Mathlib.RingTheory.Unramified.Basic
8	import Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.Basic
9	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme
10	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.FinitePresentation
11	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Smooth
12	import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Differentials.Presheaf
13	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Presheaf
14	
15	/-! # Relative Kähler differentials for schemes (presheaf form)
16	
17	This file constructs the **presheaf** of relative Kähler differentials
18	`Ω_{X/S}` of a morphism of schemes `f : X → S` and states the
19	characterisation of smoothness in terms of pointwise local freeness of
20	`Ω` on affine opens.
21	
22	The construction builds on Mathlib's ring-theoretic `KaehlerDifferential`
23	and the presheaf-of-modules differential construction
24	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`.
25	
26	## References
27	
28	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex`.
29	-/
30	
31	set_option autoImplicit false
32	
33	universe u
34	
35	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
36	
37	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
38	
39	variable {X S : Scheme.{u}}
40	
41	/-- The relative cotangent **presheaf** of a morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ S`.
42	
43	On each open `U ⊆ X`, the sections are the Kähler differential module of
44	the ring map `O_S(f(U)) → O_X(U)` induced by `f`. More precisely, we use
45	the inverse-image presheaf `f⁻¹ O_S` on `X` and the canonical map to `O_X`;
46	the Kähler differential construction then gives a presheaf of `O_X`-modules.
47	
48	Mathlib leverage: `TopCat.Presheaf.pullback` for `f⁻¹`,
49	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`
50	for the presheaf of Kähler differentials. -/
51	noncomputable def relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f : X ⟶ S) : X.PresheafOfModules :=
52	  let φ' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm
53	    f.c
54	  PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials' φ'
55	
56	/-- Localisation-compatibility identity: on each open `V ⊆ X`, the sections
57	of `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f` over `V` are *definitionally* the
58	Kähler differential module of the ring map
59	`(f⁻¹ O_S)(V) → O_X(V)`. -/
60	theorem relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_obj_kaehler (f : X ⟶ S)
61	    (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
62	    ((relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf.obj V : Type _) =
63	      CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential
64	        (((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat
65	          f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm f.c |>.app V) :=
66	  rfl
67	
68	/-- The Kähler module of a localization is subsingleton.
69	This is project-local Lemma `lem:kaehler_localization_subsingleton`. -/
70	theorem kaehler_localization_subsingleton
71	    {A L : Type u} [CommRing A] [CommRing L] [Algebra A L]
72	    (M : Submonoid A) [IsLocalization M L] :
73	    Subsingleton (Ω[L⁄A]) :=
74	  letI : Algebra.FormallyUnramified A L :=
75	    Algebra.FormallyUnramified.of_isLocalization (R := A) (Rₘ := L) M
76	  Algebra.FormallyUnramified.subsingleton_kaehlerDifferential
77	
78	/-- Tower-cancellation `LinearEquiv` for the Kähler module under a localization:
79	if `A → L` is a localization at `M ⊆ A` and `L → B` is a ring map forming a
80	scalar tower `A → L → B`, then the canonical map `Ω[B/A] → Ω[B/L]` is a
81	`B`-linear equivalence.
82	
83	This is project-local Lemma `lem:kaehler_quotient_localization_iso` and the most
84	extractable Mathlib contribution candidate of milestone M1 (candidate name
85	`KaehlerDifferential.equivOfFormallyUnramified`). -/
86	noncomputable def kaehler_quotient_localization_iso
87	    {A L B : Type u} [CommRing A] [CommRing L] [CommRing B]
88	    [Algebra A L] [Algebra A B] [Algebra L B] [IsScalarTower A L B]
89	    (M : Submonoid A) [IsLocalization M L] :
90	    Ω[B⁄A] ≃ₗ[B] Ω[B⁄L] := by
91	  haveI : Subsingleton (Ω[L⁄A]) := kaehler_localization_subsingleton M
92	  refine LinearEquiv.ofBijective (KaehlerDifferential.map A L B B) ⟨?_, ?_⟩
93	  · rw [injective_iff_map_eq_zero]
94	    intro x hx
95	    obtain ⟨y, rfl⟩ :=
96	      (KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map A L B x).mp hx
97	    -- y : B ⊗[L] Ω[L⁄A]; since Ω[L⁄A] is subsingleton, the tensor product is too
98	    haveI : Subsingleton (TensorProduct L B (Ω[L⁄A])) := by
99	      refine ⟨fun a b => ?_⟩
100	      have hzero : ∀ z : TensorProduct L B (Ω[L⁄A]), z = 0 := by
101	        intro z
102	        induction z with
103	        | zero => rfl
104	        | tmul x y =>
105	          rw [show y = (0 : Ω[L⁄A]) from Subsingleton.elim _ _, TensorProduct.tmul_zero]
106	        | add x y hx hy => rw [hx, hy, add_zero]
107	      rw [hzero a, hzero b]
108	    rw [Subsingleton.elim y 0, map_zero]
109	  · exact KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective _ _ _
110	
111	/-- Forward direction of the Jacobian criterion (algebra-Kähler form).
112	If `f : X → S` is smooth of relative dimension `n`, then for every
113	point `x ∈ X` there exist affine opens `U ⊆ S` and `V ⊆ X` with
114	`f V ⊆ U` and `x ∈ V`, on which the Kähler differential module
115	`Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ Γ(S, U)]` (over the appLE algebra structure) is a free
116	module of rank `n` over `Γ(X, V)`.
117	
118	The reverse direction (locally free Ω of rank `n` implies smooth of
119	relative dimension `n`) is **mathematically false** as stated without
120	additional deformation-theoretic input (vanishing of
121	`Algebra.H1Cotangent A B` on each chart); see the chapter
122	`Differentials.tex` for the counterexample (`Spec k → Spec k[t]`,
123	`t ↦ 0`) and the converse-direction disclosure. -/
124	theorem smooth_locally_free_omega {n : ℕ} (f : X ⟶ S)
125	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n f] :
126	    ∀ (x : X), ∃ (U : S.Opens) (V : X.Opens) (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U),
127	        x ∈ V ∧ IsAffineOpen U ∧ IsAffineOpen V ∧
128	          letI : Algebra Γ(S, U) Γ(X, V) :=
129	            (Scheme.Hom.appLE f U V e).hom.toAlgebra
130	          (Module.Free Γ(X, V) (Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ Γ(S, U)]) ∧
131	           Module.rank Γ(X, V) (Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ Γ(S, U)]) = n) := by
132	  intro x
133	  obtain ⟨U, hU, V, hV, hxV, e, hRing⟩ :=
134	    SmoothOfRelativeDimension.exists_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension (n := n) (f := f) x
135	  refine ⟨U, V, e, hxV, hU, hV, ?_, ?_⟩ <;>
136	    · algebraize [CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appLE f U V e)]
137	      haveI : Algebra.IsStandardSmooth Γ(S, U) Γ(X, V) :=
138	        Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.isStandardSmooth n
139	      haveI : Nonempty V := ⟨⟨x, hxV⟩⟩
140	      first
141	      | exact Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free_kaehlerDifferential
142	      | exact Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential n
143	
144	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

13	/-!
14	# Pullback of presheaves of modules
15	
16	Let `F : C ⥤ D` be a functor, `R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat` and `S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat` be presheaves
17	of rings, and `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R` be a morphism of presheaves of rings,
18	we introduce the pullback functor `pullback : PresheafOfModules S ⥤ PresheafOfModules R`
19	as the left adjoint of `pushforward : PresheafOfModules R ⥤ PresheafOfModules S`.
20	The existence of this left adjoint functor is obtained under suitable universe assumptions.
21	
22	From the compatibility of `pushforward` with respect to composition, we deduce
23	similar pseudofunctor-like properties of the `pullback` functors.
24	
25	-/
26	
27	@[expose] public section
28	
29	universe v v₁ v₂ v₃ v₄ u₁ u₂ u₃ u₄ u
30	
31	open CategoryTheory Limits Opposite Functor
32	
33	namespace PresheafOfModules
34	
35	section
36	
37	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
38	  {F : C ⥤ D} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R)
39	  [(pushforward.{v} φ).IsRightAdjoint]
40	
41	/-- The pullback functor `PresheafOfModules S ⥤ PresheafOfModules R` induced by
42	a morphism of presheaves of rings `S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`, defined as the left adjoint
43	functor to the pushforward, when it exists. -/
44	noncomputable def pullback : PresheafOfModules.{v} S ⥤ PresheafOfModules.{v} R :=
45	  (pushforward.{v} φ).leftAdjoint
46	
47	/-- Given a morphism of presheaves of rings `S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`, this is the adjunction
48	between associated pullback and pushforward functors on the categories
49	of presheaves of modules. -/
50	noncomputable def pullbackPushforwardAdjunction : pullback.{v} φ ⊣ pushforward.{v} φ :=
51	  Adjunction.ofIsRightAdjoint (pushforward φ)
52	
53	/-- Given a morphism of presheaves of rings `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`, this is the property
54	that the (partial) left adjoint functor of `pushforward φ` is defined
55	on a certain object `M : PresheafOfModules S`. -/
56	abbrev pullbackObjIsDefined : ObjectProperty (PresheafOfModules.{v} S) :=
57	  (pushforward φ).leftAdjointObjIsDefined
58	
59	end
60	
61	section
62	
63	variable {C D : Type u} [SmallCategory C] [SmallCategory D]
64	  {F : C ⥤ D} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R)
65	
66	/-- Given a morphism of presheaves of rings `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`, where `F : C ⥤ D`,
67	`S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat`, `R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat` and `X : C`, the (partial) left adjoint
68	functor of `pushforward φ` is defined on the object `(free S).obj (yoneda.obj X)`:
69	this object shall be mapped to `(free R).obj (yoneda.obj (F.obj X))`. -/
70	noncomputable def pushforwardCompCoyonedaFreeYonedaCorepresentableBy (X : C) :
71	    (pushforward φ ⋙ coyoneda.obj (op ((free S).obj (yoneda.obj X)))).CorepresentableBy
72	      ((free R).obj (yoneda.obj (F.obj X))) where
73	  homEquiv {M} := freeYonedaEquiv.trans
74	    (freeYonedaEquiv (M := (pushforward φ).obj M)).symm
75	  homEquiv_comp {M N} g f := freeYonedaEquiv.injective (by
76	    dsimp
77	    erw [Equiv.apply_symm_apply, freeYonedaEquiv_comp]
78	    conv_rhs => erw [freeYonedaEquiv_comp]
79	    erw [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
80	    rfl)
81	
82	lemma pullbackObjIsDefined_free_yoneda (X : C) :
83	    pullbackObjIsDefined φ ((free S).obj (yoneda.obj X)) :=
84	  (pushforwardCompCoyonedaFreeYonedaCorepresentableBy φ X).isCorepresentable
85	
86	lemma pullbackObjIsDefined_eq_top :
87	    pullbackObjIsDefined.{u} φ = ⊤ := by
88	  ext M
89	  simp only [Pi.top_apply, Prop.top_eq_true, iff_true]
90	  apply leftAdjointObjIsDefined_of_isColimit
91	    M.isColimitFreeYonedaCoproductsCokernelCofork
92	  rintro (_ | _)
93	  all_goals
94	    apply leftAdjointObjIsDefined_colimit _
95	      (fun _ ↦ pullbackObjIsDefined_free_yoneda _ _)
96	
97	instance : (pushforward.{u} φ).IsRightAdjoint :=
98	  isRightAdjoint_of_leftAdjointObjIsDefined_eq_top
99	    (pullbackObjIsDefined_eq_top φ)
100	
101	end
102	
103	section
104	
105	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
106	  {E : Type u₃} [Category.{v₃} E] {E' : Type u₄} [Category.{v₄} E']
107	
108	variable {F : C ⥤ D} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R)
109	  {G : D ⥤ E} {T : Eᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} (ψ : R ⟶ G.op ⋙ T)
110	
111	instance : (pushforward.{v} (F := 𝟭 C) (𝟙 S)).IsRightAdjoint :=
112	  isRightAdjoint_of_iso (pushforwardId.{v} S).symm
113	
114	variable (S) in
115	/-- The pullback by the identity morphism identifies to the identity functor of the
116	category of presheaves of modules. -/
117	noncomputable def pullbackId : pullback.{v} (F := 𝟭 C) (𝟙 S) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
118	  ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{v} (F := 𝟭 C) (𝟙 S))).leftAdjointIdIso (pushforwardId S)
119	
120	variable [(pushforward.{v} φ).IsRightAdjoint]
121	
122	section
123	
124	variable [(pushforward.{v} ψ).IsRightAdjoint]
125	
126	instance : (pushforward.{v} (F := F ⋙ G) (φ ≫ whiskerLeft F.op ψ)).IsRightAdjoint :=
127	  isRightAdjoint_of_iso (pushforwardComp.{v} φ ψ)
128	
129	/-- The composition of two pullback functors on presheaves of modules identifies
130	to the pullback for the composition. -/
131	noncomputable def pullbackComp :
132	    pullback.{v} φ ⋙ pullback.{v} ψ ≅
133	      pullback.{v} (F := F ⋙ G) (φ ≫ whiskerLeft F.op ψ) :=
134	  Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso
135	    (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{v} φ) (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{v} ψ)
136	    (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{v} (F := F ⋙ G) (φ ≫ whiskerLeft F.op ψ))
137	    (pushforwardComp φ ψ)
138	
139	variable {T' : E'ᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {G' : E ⥤ E'} (ψ' : T ⟶ G'.op ⋙ T')
140	  [(pushforward.{v} ψ').IsRightAdjoint]
141	
142	lemma pullback_assoc :
143	    isoWhiskerLeft _ (pullbackComp.{v} ψ ψ') ≪≫
144	      pullbackComp.{v} (G := G ⋙ G') φ (ψ ≫ whiskerLeft G.op ψ') =
145	    (associator _ _ _).symm ≪≫ isoWhiskerRight (pullbackComp.{v} φ ψ) _ ≪≫
146	        pullbackComp.{v} (F := F ⋙ G) (φ ≫ whiskerLeft F.op ψ) ψ' :=
147	  Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso_assoc _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (pushforward_assoc φ ψ ψ')
148	
149	end
150	
151	lemma pullback_id_comp :
152	    pullbackComp.{v} (F := 𝟭 C) (𝟙 S) φ =
153	      isoWhiskerRight (pullbackId S) (pullback φ) ≪≫ Functor.leftUnitor _ :=
154	  Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso_id_comp _ _ _ _ (pushforward_comp_id φ)
155	
156	lemma pullback_comp_id :
157	    pullbackComp.{v} (G := 𝟭 _) φ (𝟙 R) =
158	      isoWhiskerLeft _ (pullbackId R) ≪≫ Functor.rightUnitor _ :=
159	  Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso_comp_id _ _ _ _ (pushforward_id_comp φ)
160	
161	end
162	
163	end PresheafOfModules

287	/-- The associativity diagram of a group object is Cartesian.
288	
289	In fact, any monoid object whose associativity diagram is Cartesian can be made into a group object

291	follow from this result. -/

294	    IsPullback (μ ▷ A) ((α_ A A A).hom ≫ (A ◁ μ)) μ μ where
295	  w := by simp
296	  isLimit' := Nonempty.intro <| PullbackCone.IsLimit.mk _
297	    (fun s => lift
298	      (lift
299	        (s.snd ≫ fst _ _)
300	        (lift (s.snd ≫ fst _ _ ≫ ι) (s.fst ≫ fst _ _) ≫ μ))
301	      (s.fst ≫ snd _ _))
302	    (by
303	      refine fun s => CartesianMonoidalCategory.hom_ext _ _ ?_ (by simp)
304	      simp only [lift_whiskerRight, lift_fst]
305	      rw [← lift_lift_assoc, ← assoc, lift_comp_inv_right, lift_comp_one_left])
306	    (by
307	      refine fun s => CartesianMonoidalCategory.hom_ext _ _ (by simp) ?_
308	      simp only [lift_lift_associator_hom_assoc, lift_whiskerLeft, lift_snd]
309	      have : lift (s.snd ≫ fst _ _ ≫ ι) (s.fst ≫ fst _ _) ≫ μ =

311	        rw [← assoc s.fst, eq_lift_inv_right, lift_lift_assoc, ← assoc s.snd, lift_inv_left_eq,
312	          lift_comp_fst_snd, lift_comp_fst_snd, s.condition]
313	      rw [this, lift_lift_assoc, ← assoc, lift_comp_inv_left, lift_comp_one_right])
314	    (by
315	      intro s m hm₁ hm₂
316	      refine CartesianMonoidalCategory.hom_ext _ _ (CartesianMonoidalCategory.hom_ext _ _ ?_ ?_) ?_
317	      · simpa using hm₂ =≫ fst _ _
318	      · have h : m ≫ fst _ _ ≫ fst _ _ = s.snd ≫ fst _ _ := by simpa using hm₂ =≫ fst _ _

13	/-!
14	# The category of groups in a Cartesian monoidal category
15	
16	We define group objects in Cartesian monoidal categories.
17	
18	We show that the associativity diagram of a group object is always Cartesian and deduce that
19	morphisms of group objects commute with taking inverses.
20	
21	We show that a finite-product-preserving functor takes group objects to group objects.
22	-/
23	
24	@[expose] public section
25	
26	universe v₁ v₂ v₃ u₁ u₂ u₃ u
27	
28	open CategoryTheory Category Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory Mon MonObj
29	
30	namespace CategoryTheory
31	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] [CartesianMonoidalCategory.{v₁} C]
32	
33	/-- An additive group object internal to a cartesian monoidal category.
34	Also see the bundled `AddGrp`. -/
35	class AddGrpObj (X : C) extends AddMonObj X where
36	  /-- The negation in a group object -/
37	  neg : X ⟶ X
38	  left_neg (X) : lift neg (𝟙 X) ≫ add = toUnit _ ≫ zero := by cat_disch
39	  right_neg (X) : lift (𝟙 X) neg ≫ add = toUnit _ ≫ zero := by cat_disch
40	
41	/-- A group object internal to a cartesian monoidal category. Also see the bundled `Grp`. -/
42	@[to_additive]
43	class GrpObj (X : C) extends MonObj X where
44	  /-- The inverse in a group object -/
45	  inv : X ⟶ X
46	  left_inv (X) : lift inv (𝟙 X) ≫ mul = toUnit _ ≫ one := by cat_disch
47	  right_inv (X) : lift (𝟙 X) inv ≫ mul = toUnit _ ≫ one := by cat_disch
48
